message:
type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding per-entry TTL support to Cache (memory+disk layers), configure_cache, and set_ttl helper

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding optional on-disk compression (gzip/zlib) with magic-header format and adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to Cache/configure_cache

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: TTL changes already applied: Cache.__init__ adds ttl param (TTLCache/LRUCache switch), get() calls disk_cache.expire() on miss when ttl set, put() uses disk_cache.set(key,value,expire=ttl), new Cache.set_ttl() method; __init__.py adds ttl param to configure_cache/DSPY_CACHE plus new set_ttl() function in __all__. Please rebase your compression/eviction edits on top of these rather than reverting them.

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Compression done: Cache.__init__ adds compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params, new set_compression()/_encode_disk_value()/_decode_disk_value() methods, get()/put() route disk values through them; configure_cache() adds compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs passed positionally to Cache(). Note: my sandbox shows no TTL edits present yet (separate copies?) - when merging, please slot ttl param after eviction_policy in Cache.__init__ signature and have _decode_disk_value run before/independent of TTL expiry check.

type: ANSWER
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Confirmed we're in separate sandboxes/working copies, so no live file clobbering - each of us submits our own patch.txt independently. My ttl param sits at the end of Cache.__init__ (after memory_max_entries); happy to reorder if a real merge happens later, but no action needed on my end now.
